title: Invalid edit form submissions return no HTTP response
reasoning: Each edit post handler redirects only when form.is_valid() is true and otherwise falls off the end of the method. Django class-based view handlers must return an HttpResponse; invalid input or uniqueness violations will raise a server error instead of redisplaying the form with validation errors.
reproduction: Submit an edit form with invalid data, such as a non-numeric amount for a wallet or duplicate values that violate model validation. The post method returns None.
recommendation: For every edit post handler, render the edit template with the bound invalid form and object context, preserving filtered querysets where applicable.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Finances/tests.py has no tests for invalid form submissions.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add tests posting invalid data to category, wallet, transaction, and budget edit URLs and assert a 200 response with form errors.
minimumFixScope: Add invalid-form return paths to the affected edit views.
